content:
Do you need them physically in one inbox, or presented via thunderbird as one virtual inbox. The virtual inbox is an easy setup and lets you treat all mail from one virtual inbox and, if desired, all sent messages in one virtual sent folder. Let me know.

On your other approach, if a gmail account is forwarding to a second account, why have the gmail account in thunderbird at all? Forward it from the gmail online account.
creator: davidsk

content:
Thank you for your quick response. I would prefer them to be physically in one inbox so that I can organize them and keep the important ones in sub folders for easy retrieval.

I am not clear on what you mean about forward from the gmail account. At one point I had gmail sending to Thunderbird but it sent it to a second inbox in Thunderbird, not to my primary inbox. Then when I deleted emails from Gmail it also appeared to delete them from Thunderbird which was counter productive.

Ideally I only want one inbox and only one set of emails in that inbox.

TYIA Cindy
creator: tikatoc23

content: You cannot send to thunderbird, as it is only a client. But, you can log onto gmail online and set to send from there. The setting there allows you to send and also delete. In that way, you do NOT need the gmail account in thunderbird. see attached screenshot
creator: davidsk

content: Add additional fonts to your OS and thunderbird will use them. If you post a screenshot of whatever irritates you, I'm sure someone will respond. Thank you
creator: davidsk
